name: Grand Prize Winner Black Bean Tamale Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray a 9-inch pie plate with nonstick cooking spray and set aside.
3. Spray a medium-sized skillet with nonstick cooking spray.
4. Saute onions and green peppers until tender, about 3-5 minutes. Add turkey and cook until no longer pink, breaking the meat up into small chunks as you cook. Stir in beans, tomato sauce, and taco seasoning mix. Set aside.
5. In a medium bowl, combine cornmeal, sugar, and salt. In a separate small bowl, mix the egg, milk, and oil. Add egg mixture to cornmeal mixture and combine well. Stir in drained corn.
6. Press the cornmeal mixture into the prepared pie plate like a crumb crust. Spoon the bean mixture on top of the cornmeal "crust".
7. Bake for 20 minutes. Sprinkle grated cheese on top and bake an additional 5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted. Let stand 5 minutes before serving.
This recipe, by Greta Weingast of Benecia California, won the $10,000 Grand Prize in the July 8, 1991 Lake to Lake/Cheddar Recipe Contest. The original recipe calls for Cheddar rather than any old grated cheddar cheese.
